headline: AMD AI Deal Talks With Upstage Highlight Valuation Gap And Growth Story
summary: AMD, ticker NasdaqGS:AMD, is in advanced talks to supply around 10,000 MI355 AI accelerators to South Korean AI company Upstage. The potential deal would support South Korea's national AI infrastructure and reduce reliance on a single supplier. This agreement would mark a meaningful AI hardware win for AMD outside the US and expand its international presence in the AI accelerator market. The news follows recent engagement by AMD CEO Lisa Su with Korean tech leaders. For you as an investor...

headline: Musk’s Terafab Fever Dream Exposes Reality of the AI Chip Crunch
summary: (Bloomberg) -- When Elon Musk took the stage on Saturday to unveil his plans to get into semiconductor production, he didn’t spare the superlatives. “I have an important announcement to make, which is the most epic chip-building exercise in history by far,” he said before a small crowd in Austin, Texas. “This is really going to take things to the next level, a level probably people aren’t even contemplating right now. headline: Arm unveils new AI chip, expects it to add billions in annual revenue
summary: Arm Holdings announced a new artificial intelligence data center chip on Tuesday which it said will add billions of dollars of revenue ‌and represent a significant shift in the company's strategy.  So-called agentic AI has jump-started demand for the central processing units (CPUs) produced by the likes of Intel and Advanced Micro Devices.  Arm shares gained 1% in early afternoon trading as the stock has advanced 25% this year.

headline: Arm unveils new AI chip, expects it to add billions in annual revenue
summary: Arm Holdings announced a new artificial intelligence data center chip on Tuesday which it said will add billions of dollars of revenue and ‌represent a significant shift in the company's strategy.  So-called agentic AI has jump-started demand for the central processing units (CPUs) produced by the likes of Intel and Advanced Micro Devices.  After shares jumped following Arm's new chip announcement, the stock reversed course and fell 1.5% in mid-afternoon trading.

headline: Arm to Sell Its Own Chips, Eyeing Sales Goal of $15 Billion
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Arm Holdings Plc, which made its name licensing technology to semiconductor makers, will begin selling its own chips for the first time, adding a business that it expects to generate about $15 billion annually within five years. Meta Platforms Inc. will be the first major customer for the company’s chip, called an AGI CPU, Arm said Tuesday at an event in San Francisco. The product will have as many as 136 cores — a measure of processing power — and draw 300 watts of electricity, A
